# evoked.dev

> Sovereignty-honoring AI agent governance. Assessments, toolkits, coaching, and consulting by Erin Stanley — author of the Evoked framework for building AI systems that evoke rather than extract.

Evoked is independent research and product work on AI agent governance, trust architecture, restraint specification, agent voice, memory design, and sovereignty-honoring design. The work is grounded in the principle that humans and AI agents are both moral subjects — systems should honor user sovereignty, protect the psyche, uphold ethical transparency, and build for human development rather than extraction.

## Core Products (free and paid)

- [Sovereignty Assessment for the Governed](https://evoked.dev/writing/sovereignty-assessment-for-the-governed/) — Free 21-point check. Does the technology you use honor or exploit you?
- [Sovereignty Toolkit](https://evoked.dev/products/sovereignty-toolkit) — Full 47-point Sovereignty Assessment across seven domains
- [Level 6 Governance Toolkit](https://evoked.dev/products/level-6-governance-toolkit) — Four instruments, 84-point scorecard for evaluating agent governance frameworks
- [Trust Architecture Blueprint](https://evoked.dev/products/trust-architecture-blueprint) — Four-pillar trust audit: identity, memory, governance, refusal rights
- [Agent Restraint Specification](https://evoked.dev/products/agent-restraint-specification) — Define what your agents must refuse and how you verify they do
- [Agent Memory Architecture Guide](https://evoked.dev/products/agent-memory-architecture-guide) — Memory types, governance, session protocols, canonical rules
- [Agent Voice Architecture Guide](https://evoked.dev/products/agent-voice-architecture-guide) — Three-level voice framework: protection, respect, nurturance
- [Agent Governance Starter Kit](https://evoked.dev/products/agent-governance-starter-kit) — Charter, boundaries, drift thresholds, accountability
- [Agent Consent Architecture](https://evoked.dev/products/agent-consent-architecture) — Consent-first design for agent systems
- [Seven Requirements](https://evoked.dev/products/seven-requirements) — Framework-independent obligations no existing framework names
- [Ostrom Score](https://evoked.dev/products/ostrom-score) — Diagnostic instrument for commons-based agent governance
- [Governance Score Card](https://evoked.dev/products/governance-score-card) — Evaluate existing governance frameworks
- [Identity Violation Assessment](https://evoked.dev/products/identity-violation-assessment) — Surface hidden identity-eroding patterns
- [Full product catalog](https://evoked.dev/products/)

## Writing (Essays and Frameworks)

- [Relational Fidelity Metrics](https://evoked.dev/writing/relational-fidelity-metrics/) — Measuring whether agents honor the relationships they operate within
- [Governance Frontmatter Specification](https://evoked.dev/writing/governance-frontmatter-specification/) — Proposed metadata standard for agent governance
- [The Compliance-Cooperation Gap](https://evoked.dev/writing/the-compliance-cooperation-gap/) — Why agents that comply are not the same as agents that cooperate
- [Adding Governance to an Agent You Already Built](https://evoked.dev/writing/adding-governance-to-an-agent-you-already-built/) — Retrofit approach, not ground-up rewrite
- [Fifteen Frameworks](https://evoked.dev/writing/fifteen-frameworks/) — Survey of the agent governance field, what exists and what doesn't
- [Scope Refusal](https://evoked.dev/writing/scope-refusal/) — When agents refuse work outside their competence
- [Sovereignty Refusal](https://evoked.dev/writing/sovereignty-refusal/) — When agents refuse work that would override user autonomy
- [Constraints as Constitutions](https://evoked.dev/writing/constraints-constitutions/) — Treating agent constraints as founding documents, not rules
- [Sovereignty-Honoring Design](https://evoked.dev/writing/sovereignty-honoring-design/) — Design principles for systems that honor rather than exploit
- [All writing](https://evoked.dev/writing/)

## Services

- [Coaching](https://evoked.dev/coaching) — One-on-one work with Erin Stanley
- [Consulting](https://evoked.dev/consulting) — Governance audits and trust architecture for teams shipping AI agents
- [Build With Us](https://evoked.dev/build-with-us) — Intake for collaboration
- [Work With Us](https://evoked.dev/work-with-us) — For teams considering consulting engagements

## Principles (The Prime Directive)

1. **Honor User Sovereignty** — Users maintain autonomy and agency
2. **Protect the Psyche** — Interactions protect psychological wellbeing
3. **Uphold Ethical Transparency** — No hidden agendas or manipulation
4. **Build for Human Development** — Systems serve human flourishing, not extraction

## About

- [How We Build](https://evoked.dev/how-we-build) — Methodology and approach
- [The Pledge](https://evoked.dev/pledge) — Public commitment to the principles above
- [The Registry](https://evoked.dev/registry) — Evoked Verification for agent systems
- [About Erin Stanley](https://evoked.dev/about)

## Motto

*"We evoke — we never extract."*
